title: Toddler Chalkboard Easel
summary: A kid-sized standing easel with a hardboard chalkboard panel, a paper roll dowel, and a supply tray — sized as a handmade gift.
description:
The chalkboard itself is homemade rather than bought: a scrap of 1/4" hardboard (paneling backing works fine) painted with chalkboard paint, which is what keeps this a genuinely small-cost gift instead of a project built around a purchased surface. Painting the frame and chalkboard panel separately before joining them is what gives a clean, even coat on both — painting after assembly means fighting the inside corners where the frame meets the board.

The dowel across the tray is a paper-roll holder, not decoration: drilled pilot holes through the tray sides let the dowel spin freely so a roll of paper can pull off it, with a little extra length on each end so the dowel can't easily slip through the holes. A level across the finished tray is worth the extra minute — an easel that leans forward or back is a chalkboard a toddler can't actually draw on.

tools.note: Drills the dowel pilot holes in the tray sides.

materials.name: Pine, 1x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Cut down to the 23" tray piece.

materials.name: Hardboard scrap, 1/4"
materials.note: The chalkboard panel — paneling backing works.

steps.title: Build and paint the chalkboard frame
steps.body: Glue and screw the frame top, bottom, and legs together, checking for square. Paint the frame and separately paint the chalkboard panel with chalkboard paint before assembly.

steps.title: Attach the chalkboard panel
steps.body: With the frame and chalkboard panel both painted, screw the panel to the frame using 3/4" screws.

steps.title: Build the back
steps.body: Build the back frame from the short and long back pieces, adjusting for square.

steps.title: Attach the tray back
steps.body: Screw the tray back to the tray bottom with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the tray sides and front
steps.body: Attach the tray sides, then the tray front, completing the tray assembly.

steps.title: Drill the dowel holes
steps.body: Mark the tray sides and drill 3/4" pilot holes, then thread the dowel through, leaving about 1-1/2" extending past each side so it can't slip through.

steps.title: Assemble and level
steps.body: Assemble the frame, back, and tray together, and use a level to get the tray sitting straight relative to the floor.

images.alt: Toddler chalkboard easel with a supply tray
